nameMeanings.name: isle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from a Lincolnshire variant of   Iles  . See also   Lisle   and compare   Lill  .

variants.name: Iles
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Lisle   (see   Lyle  ).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Man
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wai, Chan, Cheuk, Kin, Kwok, Sai, Shek, Tong, Chi, Choong, Chung Yee, Hin.
Flemish and Dutch (also De Man): from Middle Dutch man ‘man, husband, vassal’ (compare Mann ).
English: variant of   Mann  .

variants.name: Mann
meanings: English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a fierce or strong man, or for a man contrasted with a boy, from Middle English, Middle High German man, German Mann ‘man’. In some cases it may have arisen as an occupational name for a servant, from the medieval use of the term to describe a person of inferior social status. The Jewish surname can be artificial.

English and German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Manno, found in Old English as Mann or Manna. This originated either as a byname or else as a short form of a compound name containing the element man ‘man’, such as Hermann .
English: habitational name from the Isle of Man.

variants.name: Mendel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erwin, Florian, Kurt, Alois, Monika, Taube.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Mendl, a diminutive of Man (see Man 9).
South German: variant of   Meindl  . This surname is also found in France (mainly Lorraine and Alsace).

variants.name: Maan
meanings: Indian (Punjab): Jat and Sikh name of unexplained etymology, borne by one of the three oldest Jat tribes to settle in the subcontinent.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 滿, 萬, 文, 閔, and 聞. See   Man   5-8.
Muslim (mainly Iraq and Syria): habitational name from any of several places called Maʿān, in Arabia and the Levant.

variants.name: Manne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Anuradha, Kiran, Kishore, Murthy, Prabhakar, Prasad, Rao.  Jewish Akiva, Shmuel, Yaakov.
Indian (Andhra Pradesh; pronounced as two syllables): unexplained.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish male personal name Mane, a variant of Man 9.

nameMeanings.name: Ragnhild
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old Norse personal name from regin ‘advice, decision’ (also, ‘the gods’) + hildr ‘battle’.

variants.name: Ragnild
meanings: From an Old Norse personal name from regin ‘advice, decision’ (also, ‘the gods’) + hildr ‘battle’.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

nameMeanings.name: Rachel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘ewe’ in Hebrew), borne by the beloved wife of Jacob and mother (after long barrenness) of Joseph (Genesis 28–35) and of Benjamin, at whose birth she died. In the Middle Ages and subsequently this was regarded as a characteristically Jewish name, but it is now also popular among Gentiles.

variants.name: Rachelle
meanings: Elaborated form of   Rachel  , as if from French, but actually a recent coinage in English.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

nameMeanings.name: affrica
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Name recorded in the U.S. since the 18th century and now favoured by African Americans conscious of their ancestral heritage.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

en.lifeSummary: Ragnhild or Rachel or affrica of isle of Man Princess was born about 1127, in Isle of Man. She married Somerled of the Isles King Of Man"the Viking". They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Ragnhild or Rachel or affrica of isle of Man Princess est née environ 1127, à Île de Man. Elle a épousé Somerled of the Isles King Of Man"the Viking". Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Ragnhild or Rachel or affrica of isle of Man Princess wurde um 1127 in Isle of Man geboren. Sie hat Somerled of the Isles King Of Man"the Viking" geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Ragnhild or Rachel or affrica of isle of Man Princess è nata il circa 1127 nel Isola di Man. Lei sposato Somerled of the Isles King Of Man"the Viking". Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia.
